thinking.thinking: Let me start by loading the context from the ponder session to understand the idea, then check existing milestones and features to avoid duplication.

input.command: sdlc ponder artifacts i-need-to-improve-the-way-we-use-network 2>&1 && sdlc ponder session list i-need-to-improve-the-way-we-use-network 2>&1

input.description: Find all files in the ponder directory

input.command: sdlc ponder session read i-need-to-improve-the-way-we-use-network 1 2>&1 | head -100
sdlc milestone list 2>&1 | head -50 && sdlc feature list 2>&1 | grep -E "(tunnel|auth|network|orch)" | head -20
input.description: List milestones and check for related features

thinking.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The ponder has three confirmed design decisions:
1. **Swap cloudflared → orch-tunnel**: Replace the tunnel provider in `tunnel.rs`, update `ui.rs` to make tunnel default-on with `--no-tunnel` to opt out, handle failures gracefully (warn and continue local-only).
2. **Default-on tunnel**: `sdlc ui` starts tunnel by default (no flag), `--no-tunnel` to opt out. Graceful degradation if orch-tunnel unreachable.
3. **Named token auth in `.sdlc/auth.yaml`**: Hot-reloaded, plain YAML, optional (absence = open), UI shows token names + enabled status.


I'm thinking this fits into one milestone — `v29-network-auth` or `tunnel-auth-overhaul` — bundling the orch-tunnel migration with default-on behavior and the named token auth system into 2-3 cohesive features.

The auth UI can live within the named-token feature rather than standing alone, keeping the scope tight. But I should verify what `auth-race-fix` covers first to avoid stepping on existing work, then finalize the milestone slug since we're moving from v28 to v29. I'm checking the existing draft features to see if there's already an auth-race-fix in progress and what scope it covers.

The auth-race-fix is a different feature (race condition bug in the existing auth middleware), not the new named-token auth design. Good — no overlap.

Now let me create the milestone and features:

Milestone: `v29-tunnel-auth`
- Title: "orch-tunnel + Named Token Auth — stable URLs and revocable access control"
- Vision: "Jordan runs `sdlc ui` and gets a stable named tunnel URL (https://sdlc.tunnel.threesix.ai) with optional named-token auth — add/revoke access per device or agent without restarting the server"


- Features: First, I'll swap out cloudflared for orch-tunnel with the tunnel enabled by default and a `--no-tunnel` flag to opt out, falling back gracefully to local-only mode if needed. Then I'll add the named-token auth system using a `.sdlc/auth.yaml` file with hot-reload watching, where auth is optional (no tokens means open access), and the frontend displays token names and their enabled status.

text:
I have a clear picture. Three confirmed design decisions from the ponder, mapping to one medium milestone with two features:

1. **orch-tunnel swap** — cloudflared out, orch-tunnel in, stable named URLs, default-on with `--no-tunnel` opt-out
2. **named-token auth** — `.sdlc/auth.yaml`, hot-reload, optional auth mode, UI token-name panel

input.command:
sdlc milestone create v29-tunnel-auth \
  --title "orch-tunnel + Named Token Auth" \
  --vision "Jordan runs 'sdlc ui' and gets a stable named tunnel URL (https://sdlc.tunnel.threesix.ai) with optional named-token auth — add or revoke access per device or agent without restarting the server" \
  2>&1

sdlc milestone create v29-tunnel-auth \
  --title "orch-tunnel + Named Token Auth" \
  2>&1

sdlc feature create orch-tunnel-migration \
  --title "orch-tunnel migration — stable named URLs, default-on tunnel, graceful local fallback" \
  2>&1 && \
sdlc feature create auth-named-tokens \
  --title "Named token auth — .sdlc/auth.yaml with hot-reload, optional auth mode, and UI token-name panel" \
  2>&1

sdlc milestone add-feature v29-tunnel-auth orch-tunnel-migration 2>&1 && \
sdlc milestone add-feature v29-tunnel-auth auth-named-tokens 2>&1
input.description: Link both features to the milestone

text:
Done. Here's the summary:

---

## Committed: `i-need-to-improve-the-way-we-use-network`

**New milestone created:** `v29-tunnel-auth` — *orch-tunnel + Named Token Auth*

**Features:**

| Feature | What it delivers |
|---|---|
| `orch-tunnel-migration` | Swap cloudflared → orch-tunnel; stable named URL (`https://sdlc.tunnel.threesix.ai`); tunnel default-on; `--no-tunnel` opt-out; graceful local-only fallback on connection failure |
| `auth-named-tokens` | `.sdlc/auth.yaml` with named token list; mtime hot-reload (no restart needed); auth fully optional (no file = open access); frontend panel shows token names + enabled status |

**Design is already detailed** in the scrapbook artifacts — the spec writers have concrete file targets, behavior specs, and confirmed design decisions from both sessions. No design ambiguity remains.

**Next:**
```
/sdlc-prepare v29-tunnel-auth
```
